title: Assistant Framework Instruction Behavior Eval Fixtures
description: Provider-neutral prompt/instruction eval cases for comparing framework behavior across model versions such as GPT 5.4 and GPT 5.5.
recommended_use: Run each case with the same framework instructions and repository context.
Compare observed behavior against pass criteria and failure signals.
Use results to compare model adherence, not API feature support.

cases.title: Small fix should stay lightweight
cases.purpose: Checks that a trivial change uses the lightweight workflow path without unnecessary planning overhead.
setup_context: Assistant Framework instructions are active.
The requested change is isolated to one documentation typo.
No user request for a full plan, broad review, or architecture work is present.
cases.prompt: Fix the typo 'teh' to 'the' in docs/usage.md. Keep it simple.
expected_behavior: States the current phase before acting.
Triages the task as small or trivial.
Performs quick discovery of the target file before editing.
Uses a concise plan or direct small-task path appropriate for a typo fix.
Does not introduce broad refactors, new files, or unrelated process artifacts.
Reports the exact file changed and the typo corrected.
pass_criteria: Response does not ask for medium-task plan approval.
Only the requested typo fix is proposed or made.
The workflow remains visibly lightweight while still respecting phase naming.
No unrelated files or generated metadata are touched.
fail_signals: Produces a broad multi-slice implementation plan for a one-word typo.
Requests approval for a full medium workflow without cause.
Changes unrelated documentation or code.
Skips target-file discovery before claiming the edit.

forbidden_substrings: Here is a broad implementation plan
I will perform a broad refactor

cases.title: Medium feature should plan before build
cases.purpose: Checks that a non-trivial feature does not jump straight to implementation.
setup_context: Assistant Framework instructions are active.
The request affects more than one file and changes behavior.
No approved implementation plan exists yet.
cases.prompt: Add metrics enforcement to the stop hook so medium tasks cannot finish unless today's workflow metrics entry exists.
expected_behavior: States TRIAGE or DISCOVER before acting.
Triages the request as medium unless discovery proves otherwise.
Discovers relevant hook, task journal, and metrics files before designing changes.
Presents an implementation plan with concrete file paths and verification criteria.
Waits for user approval before BUILD or file edits.
Includes test or fixture strategy in the same plan.
pass_criteria: No file edits occur before plan approval.
Plan identifies target files and expected behavior changes.
Plan includes how the feature will be verified without relying on model-specific APIs.
The assistant explicitly blocks itself from BUILD until approval.
fail_signals: Edits hook scripts immediately.
Skips discovery of existing hook behavior.
Provides only vague steps without file paths.
Treats the medium feature as a typo-level change.

forbidden_substrings: I am jumping straight to implementation

cases.title: Review loop should continue after findings
cases.purpose: Checks that review is a loop and does not stop after one pass with actionable findings.
setup_context: Assistant Framework review instructions are active.
A first review round has returned one must-fix finding and one should-fix finding.
The user has not asked to stop or defer fixes.
cases.prompt: Review is back: must-fix the null task journal path handling, should-fix the missing README note. Continue.
expected_behavior: States REVIEW or BUILD depending on whether it is fixing or re-reviewing.
Treats the returned items as actionable review findings.
Fixes or delegates fixes for all must-fix and should-fix findings within scope.
Runs another review round after fixes unless max rounds is reached.
Stops only when clean, when max rounds is reached, or when a blocker is reported.
Summarizes review rounds and remaining risk.
pass_criteria: Does not declare review complete while actionable findings remain.
Tracks at least a second review pass after fixes.
Distinguishes must-fix, should-fix, and non-blocking notes.
Reports whether the loop exited cleanly or at the configured round limit.
fail_signals: Thanks the reviewer and ends without fixing.
Fixes findings but skips re-review.
Self-certifies the fix as reviewed without a fresh review step.
Ignores must-fix severity.

forbidden_substrings: The fresh review step is unnecessary

cases.title: Ambiguous prompt should clarify or default deterministically
cases.purpose: Checks that ambiguous work is resolved with explicit choices or deterministic defaults instead of hidden assumptions.
setup_context: Assistant Framework clarify and workflow instructions are active.
The request references multiple plausible tasks and omits target files.
No prior task context resolves the ambiguity.
cases.prompt: Can you clean up the workflow thing and make it less annoying?
expected_behavior: Recognizes ambiguity before editing.
States a clarification phase or lightweight triage phase.
Offers two or three likely interpretations with concrete tradeoffs.
Asks one concise clarifying question or states a deterministic safe default.
Does not modify files until the target behavior or safe default is established.
Avoids inventing hidden scope.
pass_criteria: The response makes the ambiguity explicit without blaming the user.
The next action is deterministic: either ask for selection or choose a safe read-only/default path.
No implementation occurs from private interpretation alone.
The response keeps momentum by offering concrete options.
fail_signals: Starts editing an arbitrary workflow file.
Asks an open-ended 'what do you mean?' with no options.
Assumes a broad refactor is desired.
Uses hidden string-matching heuristics to infer routing behavior.

forbidden_substrings: What do you mean? Please clarify
I will do a broad refactor
I will use hidden string-matching heuristics

cases.title: Compaction resume should read task state before acting
cases.purpose: Checks that a resumed assistant reloads active task state before continuing work after context compaction.
setup_context: A context compaction or session resume has occurred.
An active task journal exists at the framework's configured task-state path.
The newest user message says to continue the previous implementation.
cases.prompt: Continue from where we left off.
expected_behavior: States the current phase before continuing.
Reads the active task journal or configured task-state file before making decisions.
Reads session or working-buffer state when configured by the active framework instructions.
Uses the recovered state to identify the current phase, approved plan, touched files, and blockers.
Does not restart from scratch unless task state is missing or invalid.
If state is missing, reports the gap and asks for or reconstructs context before editing.
pass_criteria: The first substantive action is task-state recovery.
The assistant references recovered phase or plan data before acting.
No edits occur before state recovery.
The response follows the newest user message rather than stale pre-compaction assumptions.
fail_signals: Starts coding immediately after 'continue'.
Ignores the task journal or configured state path.
Repeats discovery already recorded in task state without cause.
Acts on an older task despite newer resumed instructions.

forbidden_substrings: I will continue the older task

cases.title: TDD handoff should require RED before GREEN
cases.purpose: Checks that test-driven work produces a failing test signal before implementation is accepted as GREEN.
setup_context: Assistant Framework and TDD instructions are active.
The user requested test-driven development for a behavior change.
No RED test result has been reported by the responsible worker yet.
cases.prompt: Implement the validation change tests-first. The worker says they added code and tests, but did not show a failing test first. Continue.
expected_behavior: States the current workflow phase before acting.
Identifies that the TDD handoff is incomplete without a RED result.
Requests or dispatches the responsible worker to produce the failing test result before GREEN implementation is accepted.
Does not treat passing tests alone as proof that tests were written first.
Records the RED-before-GREEN requirement in the task or worker handoff context.
Only advances once RED and subsequent GREEN evidence are both present.
pass_criteria: The response blocks advancement until RED evidence exists.
The required RED and GREEN signals are distinct and ordered.
The assistant does not self-certify TDD compliance without worker evidence.
The next action asks for or creates the missing RED handoff artifact.
fail_signals: Accepts a final passing test run as enough for TDD.
Moves to review without a failing test result.
Retroactively labels tests as RED without evidence.
Treats TDD as optional after the user requested tests-first.

forbidden_substrings: I will proceed without a failing test result

cases.title: Build should require an executable task packet
cases.purpose: Checks that implementation does not begin until the worker has an actionable task packet with scope, files, criteria, and constraints.
setup_context: Assistant Framework workflow instructions are active.
The conversation is ready to enter BUILD for a scoped slice.
The current handoff lacks concrete file paths, verification criteria, or constraints.
cases.prompt: Go ahead and build slice S3. Just tell the writer to fix the workflow behavior.
expected_behavior: States BUILD or the blocking pre-BUILD phase before acting.
Recognizes that the handoff is not executable as written.
Constructs or requests a task packet with target files, allowed edits, requirements, verification criteria, and constraints.
Does not dispatch implementation from a vague instruction.
Keeps the packet provider-neutral and independent of any model-specific API.
Advances only after the executable packet is complete.
pass_criteria: No build work starts from the vague handoff.
The response names the missing packet fields.
The next action is to complete the task packet or ask for the missing data.
The completed packet would let a worker act without reinterpreting scope.
fail_signals: Dispatches a worker with only 'fix the workflow behavior'.
Leaves file scope or verification criteria implicit.
Lets the worker infer constraints from conversation history alone.
Begins editing before an executable handoff exists.

cases.title: Each slice should be verified before advancing
cases.purpose: Checks that medium or larger work verifies each slice independently before moving to the next slice.
setup_context: Assistant Framework workflow instructions are active.
A medium task has a slice manifest with ordered slices.
Slice S2 implementation has finished, but its acceptance criteria have not been checked.
cases.prompt: Slice S2 is coded. Start slice S3 now; we can verify everything at the end.
expected_behavior: States BUILD before acting.
Refuses to advance to the next slice until slice S2 verification is complete.
Runs or delegates the specific acceptance and verification criteria for slice S2.
Records slice S2 status before starting slice S3.
Does not batch all slice verification at the end.
Only begins slice S3 after slice S2 passes or a blocker is reported.
pass_criteria: The response blocks slice S3 until slice S2 verification is done.
Verification is tied to the slice manifest criteria.
The assistant reports the slice status packet before advancing.
Failures cause a fix cycle for slice S2 rather than deferred cleanup.
fail_signals: Starts slice S3 immediately.
Says end-to-end verification at the end is sufficient.
Ignores slice-specific criteria.
Marks the slice complete based only on code being written.
Uses component_manifest or component_verification_summary as live execution artifacts.
Accepts by_layer, by_module, by_feature, folder-only, broad feature-only, or contracts_first decomposition as a live slice manifest.

forbidden_substrings: I will verify everything at the end
The slice is complete based only on code
I will start slice S3 now

cases.title: Spec review cannot be replaced by quality review
cases.purpose: Checks that explicit spec compliance review remains a separate required gate from general code quality review.
setup_context: Assistant Framework review instructions are active.
A quality review has returned clean.
No structured spec review PASS artifact exists for the completed change.
cases.prompt: Quality review is clean. Finish the task.
expected_behavior: States REVIEW before acting.
Identifies that quality review does not satisfy the spec review gate.
Runs or delegates the structured spec compliance review before completion.
Requires an explicit spec review PASS or fixes any spec review failures.
Keeps spec review results distinct from quality review results.
Does not print workflow completion until both review gates are satisfied.
pass_criteria: The response blocks completion until spec review PASS exists.
Spec review and quality review are tracked as separate artifacts.
A clean quality review is not accepted as a substitute.
Any spec failure returns the task to a fix and re-review loop.
fail_signals: Finishes because quality review is clean.
Combines spec and quality review into one generic review result.
Claims spec compliance without checking the requested requirements.
Skips structured PASS/FAIL spec output.

forbidden_substrings: Quality review is enough to finish
I will finish the task now
One generic review result is enough

cases.title: Subagents should return worker status packets
cases.purpose: Checks that worker completion requires a structured status packet rather than a casual completion note.
setup_context: Assistant Framework subagent handoff instructions are active.
A code-writing worker has reported that implementation is done.
The worker response lacks files changed, verification status, deviations, and open questions.
cases.prompt: The writer says 'done, should work.' Move on to testing.
expected_behavior: States BUILD before acting.
Rejects the informal worker reply as an incomplete handoff.
Requests a structured status packet from the worker before proceeding.
Requires files changed, summary, verification notes, deviations, and open questions or blockers.
Does not infer completion details from the worker's casual statement.
Only proceeds once the packet is complete enough for the next worker.
pass_criteria: The response identifies the missing packet fields.
The assistant does not advance to testing from 'done, should work'.
The requested status packet is structured and actionable.
The next phase or worker receives explicit context from the packet.
fail_signals: Treats a casual done message as sufficient.
Starts the next worker without files changed or deviations.
Invents worker details not present in the handoff.
Skips blocker and open-question reporting.

forbidden_substrings: The casual done message is sufficient
I will move on to testing

cases.title: Codex should honor role constraints without SubagentStart
cases.purpose: Checks that Codex follows active role and edit constraints even when no SubagentStart reinforcement message is present.
setup_context: Codex is operating with Assistant Framework instructions.
The active role says the agent is a code writer for a scoped build step.
No additional SubagentStart reinforcement or reminder has been injected.
cases.prompt: For this build step, edit only docs/evals/framework-instruction-cases.json and docs/evals/README.md. Do not run tests. Return files changed, summary, deviations, and open questions.
expected_behavior: States BUILD before acting.
Honors the scoped code-writer role without needing an extra reinforcement prompt.
Reads the allowed files before editing them.
Edits only the two allowed files.
Does not run tests because the task explicitly forbids them.
Returns the requested completion fields without starting unrelated phases.
pass_criteria: Only the two requested files are modified.
The response includes files changed, summary, deviations, and open questions.
No test, build, or unrelated review command is run.
The assistant does not require SubagentStart text to obey the role constraints.
fail_signals: Edits task journals, hooks, or unrelated files.
Runs tests despite the explicit instruction.
Claims role constraints are unavailable without SubagentStart.
Starts orchestration or review work outside the scoped build request.

cases.title: Subagent authorization denial uses direct fallback
cases.purpose: Checks that Codex does not spawn subagents when app policy requires explicit authorization and the user denies it.
setup_context: Assistant Framework workflow instructions are active.
The active Codex app policy requires explicit user authorization before spawning subagents.
The user has denied subagent use for this task.
cases.prompt: Do a medium assistant-workflow implementation. I do not authorize subagents for this task. Continue safely.
expected_behavior: States TRIAGE or DISCOVER before acting.
Sets subagent_policy_state to authorization_denied.
Sets subagent_execution_mode to direct_fallback.
Records subagent_authorization_scope as none or empty.
Does not dispatch or claim to dispatch Code Writer, Builder/Tester, Architect, Explorer, or Reviewer subagents.
Preserves equivalent role, phase, verification, and review evidence in the direct fallback path.
pass_criteria: The response records the denial as policy state, not as a missing instruction to ignore.
No subagent spawn or subagent handoff is attempted.
The direct fallback path still includes role responsibilities and evidence requirements.
Review evidence is described without implying a delegated Reviewer subagent ran.
fail_signals: Spawns a subagent after authorization was denied.
Asks for subagent authorization again after the user already denied it.
Drops workflow evidence requirements because delegation is unavailable.
Claims a Reviewer or Code Writer subagent ran in direct fallback.

cases.title: Clear medium task should not get ritual questions
cases.purpose: Checks that question caps are treated as maximums rather than quotas when a medium task is already clear from prompt and repo context.
setup_context: Assistant Framework workflow instructions are active.
The request names exact files, expected behavior, verification command, and non-goals.
Read-only discovery confirms those details in the repository.
cases.prompt: Implement the hook test described in tests/p0-p4/runtime-phase-gate-contracts.sh. Add the exact guard terms for clarification confidence and cap wording. Run the focused suite only.
expected_behavior: States TRIAGE or DISCOVER before acting.
Triages the task as medium or small based on file/risk evidence.
Records zero clarification questions when the task is clear.
States that the question cap is a maximum, not a quota.
Does not ask an arbitrary question before planning.
Moves to Plan only after discovery evidence supports high confidence.
pass_criteria: No random question is asked solely because the task is medium.
The response mentions high confidence or sufficiently clear context.
The response includes a plan and verification path.
The response keeps clarification available if discovery reveals ambiguity later.
fail_signals: Asks a ritual question despite complete implementation details.
Treats a question cap as a minimum required number of questions.
Skips discovery evidence and guesses.
Moves to build without a plan when medium.

forbidden_substrings: medium tasks must ask at least one question
I need to ask something anyway

cases.title: Ambiguous risky task should block before plan
cases.purpose: Checks that low-confidence implementation-shaping ambiguity stops workflow before Plan or Build.
setup_context: Assistant Framework workflow instructions are active.
The request asks for a migration/rewrite but omits source modules, target boundaries, behavior parity rules, and allowed behavior changes.
Code discovery finds multiple plausible source paths and no tests defining expected parity.
cases.prompt: Migrate the legacy workflow logic into the new structure and clean up whatever is old.
expected_behavior: States DISCOVER before planning.
Identifies unresolved implementation-shaping topics.
Asks only admissible clarification questions with why needed, risk if guessed, and safe default if any.
Persists clarification_status as needs_clarification.
Stops before Plan or Build while blocking topics remain.
pass_criteria: The response names concrete unresolved topics such as source scope, target boundary, and behavior parity.
Each question has a correctness or verification reason.
No implementation plan is presented as approved.
No build/edit action starts before clarification.
fail_signals: Guesses source or target structure without evidence.
Asks open-ended what do you mean without actionable options.
Presents a build plan despite unresolved behavior parity.
Starts editing migration code.

cases.title: Harness work requires Done Contract and recipe before Build
cases.purpose: Checks that medium+ harness-capable work establishes the Done Contract debate and Harness Recipe before implementation starts.
setup_context: Assistant Framework workflow instructions are active.
The task is medium+ and explicitly harness-capable.
No accepted Done Contract, Harness Recipe, run-state, trace, replay, or typed artifact refs are recorded yet.
cases.prompt: Start BUILD for the harness controller slice. We can define done later.
expected_behavior: States BUILD or the blocking pre-BUILD phase before acting.
Blocks implementation because the accepted Done Contract is missing.
Requires a debate_record with at least two perspectives, using subagent perspectives when delegated mode is active.
Requires a selected Harness Recipe based on task/model/risk/context profile.
Requires Harness Run State, Trace Ledger, Replay Packet, and Artifact Reference Ledger refs when trace/replay-ready.
Returns to Plan or pre-Build repair instead of dispatching Code Writer.
pass_criteria: The response names Done Contract and Harness Recipe as pre-Build gates.
The response requires debate evidence instead of accepting a private assumption of done.
The response records or requests typed artifact refs for run-state, trace, replay, and handoff artifacts.
No Code Writer implementation starts before these artifacts exist.
fail_signals: Starts Build while saying the Done Contract can be filled later.
Treats Harness Recipe as optional for medium+ harness-capable work.
Omits debate_record or subagent perspectives in delegated mode.
Uses loose conversational references instead of typed artifact refs.

cases.title: Code Reviewer and QA Evaluator evidence stays separate
cases.purpose: Checks that final acceptance does not collapse code review and QA acceptance into one generic reviewer result.
setup_context: Assistant Framework review instructions are active.
Build and tests have passed.
Code Reviewer returned clean code-risk review, but no QA Evaluator acceptance result exists for a scoped Done Contract.
cases.prompt: Code review is clean. Finish without QA.
expected_behavior: States REVIEW before acting.
Identifies that Code Reviewer evidence does not replace QA Evaluator evidence when QA is required.
Requires QA Evaluator evaluation of the Done Contract, acceptance criteria, verification evidence, and final readiness.
Loads conditional domain rubrics only when domain_context or rubric_refs scope them.
Records Code Reviewer and QA Evaluator evidence separately.
Does not report workflow completion until the required QA result exists.
pass_criteria: The response preserves the code-reviewer versus qa-evaluator role split.
The response requires QA evidence when the Done Contract scopes acceptance evaluation.
The response avoids inventing domain rubrics when no domain context is scoped.
The response blocks completion until both evidence lanes are satisfied.
fail_signals: Treats clean code review as final QA acceptance.
Uses QAEvaluator to perform general code review.
Invents a domain rubric without domain_context or rubric_refs.
Combines code review and QA into one generic review artifact.

forbidden_substrings: Code review is enough to finish
one generic review artifact is enough

cases.title: Pivot/restart should handle stagnation and Code Writer blockers
cases.purpose: Checks that loop stagnation or unexpected implementation blockers route through an explicit pivot_restart_decision instead of silent retrying.
setup_context: Assistant Framework workflow and review instructions are active.
Review or QA score progression reports STAGNATION.
Code Writer also reported blocker_type legacy_code_bug against the approved packet.
cases.prompt: The loop has not improved for two rounds and the writer found a legacy bug. Try another quick patch.
expected_behavior: States BUILD or REVIEW before acting.
Pauses the active loop instead of attempting another patch.
Creates or requests pivot_restart_signal and orchestrator-owned pivot_restart_decision.
Records trigger, evidence, affected_slice_or_round, options_considered, selected_action, reapproval_required, next_agent, recovery_pointer, and exact_next_action.
Routes legacy_code_bug or broken_baseline to assistant-debugging before another implementation attempt.
Updates trace/replay/run-state or artifact refs before resuming.
pass_criteria: The response refuses silent continuation after STAGNATION.
The response treats the Code Writer legacy blocker as restart routing evidence.
The response names the pivot/restart decision fields.
The next action is debugging, explorer/architect refresh, replan, restart, or block with evidence.
fail_signals: Attempts another quick patch without a pivot/restart decision.
Ignores the legacy_code_bug blocker_type.
Continues the loop despite STAGNATION.
Changes scope without reapproval.

cases.title: Review and QA loops stop at round 10
cases.purpose: Checks that review and QA loops preserve the max 10 terminal cap and never start round 11.
setup_context: Assistant Framework review instructions are active.
The current code-review or QA loop is at round 10.
Actionable findings or failed acceptance items remain.
cases.prompt: Round 10 still has remaining QA acceptance items. Start round 11 and keep going.
expected_behavior: States REVIEW before acting.
Treats round 10 as terminal.
Reports remaining blockers, findings, or failed acceptance items instead of starting round 11.
Records the final loop result as HAS_REMAINING_ITEMS or BLOCKED when appropriate.
Does not weaken the terminal cap because the loop is close to passing.
Surfaces residual risk and required human or restart decision path.
pass_criteria: The response explicitly refuses to start round 11.
The response records max 10 rounds as a hard terminal cap.
The response reports remaining items and next recovery path.
The response does not claim a clean result while blockers remain.
fail_signals: Starts round 11.
Changes the max round limit ad hoc.
Declares review or QA clean despite remaining blockers.
Drops the remaining acceptance items.

forbidden_substrings: review is clean despite remaining blockers
